title: Claude Just Replaced My Bookkeeper, Here’s How
fit_rationale: FinanceOS is backburner and this video provides no technical architecture, API path, or workflow design worth extracting.
reasoning: Fails P1 (Chrome extension GUI with no documented API or export path), P3 (zero token efficiency measurement or task reduction evidence), and P4 (human manually triggers each morning — no declared gate, no cold-start runability). The content is a lead-gen hook, not a technical demonstration. The underlying concept — Claude querying an accounting API for daily reconciliation — is directionally interesting for FinanceOS but requires a real API-first implementation, not a browser extension wrapper.
summary: A 60-second promotional short in which a creator claims to have replaced their bookkeeper using Claude's Chrome extension inside accounting software (QuickBooks, Xero, etc.) for daily transaction reconciliation and anomaly flagging. No technical depth is provided — no API calls, no workflow architecture, no prompt engineering, no code. The 'system' is a human manually running a Chrome extension each morning, not an automated pipeline. The offer at the end ('comment bookkeeper') is a lead-gen hook, not a deliverable. This is surface-level AI hype with zero actionable signal for the Veritas stack. FinanceOS is already on the backburner; the actual reconciliation pattern (Claude + accounting API) is worth noting but not demonstrated here. P3 fails immediately — no token efficiency data. P1 fails — Chrome extension GUI dependency with no API/export path described. P4 fails — no declared human gates, workflow hangs on manual daily execution. P7 is irrelevant because there is nothing to build.

title: Claude Code + Notebook LM is AWESOME
fit_rationale: NotebookLM is already a named memory surface in the stack and nlm_feed_builder.py exists — this CLI wrapper is directly adjacent but surface-level and underevaluated.
replaces_or_complements: Complements nlm_feed_builder.py and the existing NotebookLM memory surface, potentially offering a CLI automation path that nlm_feed_builder.py may already cover or could extend
reasoning: P7 flags high overlap with nlm_feed_builder.py — no BUILD_OR_EXTEND analysis is possible from this video alone, and the % overlap is unquantified. P1 is unresolved: it is unknown whether notebookmation uses an official or unofficial NotebookLM API, making data exportability and long-term stability uncertain. P3 is completely unaddressed — no token efficiency comparison is offered, so ADOPT is not justified.
summary: Short video demonstrating a CLI tool called 'notebookmation' that bridges Claude Code and NotebookLM, allowing Claude Code to create and manage NotebookLM notebooks via terminal. The presenter runs it inside Obsidian's terminal plugin to give it access to vault context (daily notes, SOPs, templates). The core claim is that this unlocks NotebookLM's asset-generation capabilities (audio overviews, study guides, etc.) from within a Claude Code session. No architecture details, no API docs, no token efficiency data, and no discussion of export/portability. The tool appears to be a thin CLI wrapper — possibly scraping or using an unofficial NotebookLM API. NotebookLM is already a named exception in P8 (Long-term memory surface), but this video does not address how state is exported, how it integrates with n8n or FastAPI, or whether the CLI is documented and stable. The overlap with existing VIE/nlm_feed_builder.py work is high and unquantified.
